title: Navarretia atractyloides
taxonomy: Navarretia atractyloides is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae. It is a member of the genus Navarretia, which is a group of plants native to North America. Navarretia atractyloides is further classified as a dicotyledonous plant, with a two-part flower structure and a characteristic leaf arrangement. The plant's classification is also supported by its phylogenetic relationships with other species within the Polemoniaceae family. This classification helps to identify and understand the evolutionary relationships among different plant species.

intro: Navarretia atractyloides, also known as Clarkia or Clarkia amoena, is a flowering plant species belonging to the evening primrose family (Onagraceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as infections and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Navarretia atractyloides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties.
